---
title: "Figure 5 (macro_IR) — migration of the sample-distortion peak with instrument noise, at 10 and 20 channels"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Densified noise axis at low channel number (new runs: noise 0.05/0.2/0.5 added to 0.1/1/10). At few channels
the gating emission is most non-Gaussian, so the per-sample distortion (Jensen gap between J and F) peaks at an
intermediate interval where the Poissonian gating noise and the Gaussian instrument noise are balanced. As the
instrument noise grows, the balance point moves to longer intervals: the peak Delta* should migrate to the
right. This figure shows the sample distortion vs interval at N_ch = 10 and 20 for every available noise, with
the per-curve maximum marked, to read off the migration. macro_IR, 1c2ae6f, Gaussian_Sample_Distortion diagonal.
Noise coverage differs by N_ch (10: 0.1-10 ; 20: 0.05-0.5).
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
SAMP     <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion"
NOISES   <- c("0.05", "0.1", "0.2", "0.5", "1", "10")
NCH      <- c(10, 20)
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

read_noise <- function(z) {
  fs <- Sys.glob(sprintf("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_%s_battery_pool_G.csv", z))
  if (!length(fs)) return(NULL)
  map_dfr(fs, ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)) %>% mutate(noise = z)
}
d <- map_dfr(NOISES, read_noise) %>%
  filter(component_path == SAMP, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX, Num_ch %in% NCH) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)], nz = as.numeric(noise)) %>%
  group_by(param_name, noise, nz, Num_ch, interval_in_tau) %>%
  summarise(D = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(noisef = factor(noise, levels = NOISES),
         nch_lab = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCH)),
         param   = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))

# refined interior peak Delta* (vertex of a log-log parabola through the 3 points around the argmax);
# peaks pinned at the interval-grid edge are left unmarked (censored, not resolved).
peak_iv <- function(df) {
  df <- df %>% arrange(interval_in_tau); i <- which.max(df$D)
  edge <- (i == 1) || (i == nrow(df)); Dstar <- NA_real_; Dpk <- df$D[i]
  if (!edge) {
    s <- df[(i - 1):(i + 1), ]; x <- log10(s$interval_in_tau); y <- log10(pmax(s$D, 1e-6))
    m <- lm(y ~ poly(x, 2, raw = TRUE)); a <- coef(m)[3]
    if (is.finite(a) && a < 0) {
      xv <- -coef(m)[2] / (2 * a)
      if (xv >= min(x) && xv <= max(x)) Dstar <- 10^xv else edge <- TRUE
    } else edge <- TRUE
  }
  tibble(interval_in_tau = if (is.na(Dstar)) df$interval_in_tau[i] else Dstar, D = Dpk, edge = edge)
}
pk <- d %>% group_by(param, param_name, nch_lab, Num_ch, noise, noisef, nz) %>%
  group_modify(~ peak_iv(.x)) %>% ungroup()
```

```{r migration-table}
cat("=== peak interval Delta* of the sample distortion, per (param, Nch, noise) ===\n")
pk %>% transmute(param_name, Num_ch, noise = nz, Dstar = interval_in_tau, Dmax = round(D, 3)) %>%
  arrange(param_name, Num_ch, noise) %>%
  filter(param_name %in% c("off", "on")) %>% as.data.frame() %>% print()
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 8.0, fig.height = 8.5}
g <- ggplot(d, aes(interval_in_tau, D, colour = noisef, group = noisef)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = filter(pk, !edge), size = 2.6, shape = 21, stroke = .7, fill = "white") +
  facet_grid(param ~ nch_lab, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(param = label_parsed, nch_lab = label_parsed)) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(.01, .1, 1)) +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = "instrument\nnoise", end = .92, option = "C") +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]~"  (sampling interval in relaxation times)"),
       y = "sample distortion (diagonal)  —  open circle marks the peak") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_IR_sample_peak_migration.pdf", g, width = 8.0, height = 8.5)
```
